title: Conjecture 2 (Dvořák and Mnich)
statement_text: There exists a real number $c < 3$ such that every planar graph of girth at least five has fractional chromatic number at most $c$.
context_text: Dvořák, Sereni and Volec [3] conjectured that triangle-free plane graphs without separating 4-cycles cannot have fractional chromatic number arbitrarily close to 3. They remarked that a key step would be to prove this for planar graphs of girth at least five, a special case previously conjectured by Dvořák and Mnich [2]. The present paper's stated purpose is to establish Conjecture 2 for graphs with bounded maximum degree.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 2 for planar graphs of girth at least five with maximum degree at most $\Delta$: such graphs are $(6M_\Delta : 2M_\Delta+1)$-colorable and thus have fractional chromatic number at most $3 - \frac{3}{2M_\Delta+1}$.
paper_title: Fractional coloring of planar graphs of girth five

title: Informal conjecture on improved color ratio in Theorem 5
statement_text: It might be possible to improve the ratio of extra colors assigned to the vertex $x$ in Theorem 5 a bit; e.g., it could be true that there exists a coloring by subsets of $\{1, \ldots, 9\}$ such that all vertices get at least three colors and $x$ gets five.
context_text: Following Theorem 5, the authors observe that the ratio cannot be improved arbitrarily: when $G$ is the graph obtained from the wheel with five spokes by subdividing each spoke once, $x$ is the hub, and $\varphi$ is a coloring by subsets of $\{1,\ldots,k\}$ with every vertex receiving at least 3 colors, then $|\varphi(x)| \leq \frac{k}{3} + \frac{2k}{9}$. They nonetheless suggest a modest improvement may be achievable.
notes: PDF source — doubly hedged language ('might be possible', 'could be true'); included because it specifies a concrete mathematical claim. The bound formula in the surrounding text may be garbled.
paper_title: Fractional coloring of planar graphs of girth five
